rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC SAND & GRAVEL, INC., VS. MITCHELL ENGINEERING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss First Amended Coimplaint And Strike Answer To X-Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 26, 2009, line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT MITCHELL ENGINEERING'S Motion To Dismiss First Amended Coimplaint And Strike Answer To Cross-Complaint. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE JR VS. JOYCE CONDE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Sfha, Et Al Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 26, 2009, line 18, SFHA, et al, Motion to Dimiss. Motion is Granted. This case was filed to relitigate Case # 438600, and to frustrate or abuse process against the defendants. This complaint is a sham pleading, and is dismissed. See Neal vs. Bank of America (1949) 93 Cal.App. 2nd 678. =(302/CWW/AY)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK J GABRIELLI II VS. GLOBAL CROWN CAPITAL, LLC et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 26, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT GLOBAL CROWN CAPITAL, LLC'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Motion is granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD P. HACK VS. CITIBANK, N.A. CITI
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 26, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT CITIBANK (SOUTH DAKOTA) N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled. Ten days leave to answer. =(302/CWW/FC)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LIMPERIS VS. JIMMY JEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Deft'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltf'S Verified First Amended Complaint;Request For First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 26, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT ROBERT MCCURN'S Motion To Strike Portions of Plaintiff's Verified First Amended Complaint. Motion to strike is granted in part, and denied in part. Motion is granted as to paragraph 1, page 2, lines 4-9: "Jen has been referred ....... described in this complaint." Improper and misleading, CCP section 436. Motion is denied as to remainder. A discretionary penalty is not barred by the Statute of Limitations. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LIMPERIS VS. JIMMY JEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 26, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT JIMMY JEN, DELTA DESIGN, LLC, and, BAY AREA AFFORDABLE HOUSING LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled, ten days leave to answer. The first cause of action is pled with sufficient particularity. The sixth cause of action does not require plaintiff plead he is liable to a third party. The second and third causes of action are subject to a three year statute of limitation. See tentative ruling on McCurn's demurrer. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LIMPERIS VS. JIMMY JEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 26, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT ROBERT MCCURN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled. Ten days leave to answer. The second and third causes of action are governed by a three year statute of limitations, CCP section 338(b) for actions for injury to real property. (See Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. vs. Sparks Construction, Inc. (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1135, 1150.) The first and sixth causes of action properly state claims. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF NEW YORK AS TRUSTEE FOR THE VS. ANTHONY SMITH-WINTERS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 26, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT ANTHONY SMITH-WINTERS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiffs fail to raise a triable issue of material fact. The Court adopts the reasons and facts as set forth in defendant's proposed order granting motion for summary judgment. Request for attorney fees is denied. SFAC section 37.10B(c)(6) is unconstitutional and violative of The Equal Protection Clause, in that it authorizes attorney fees to a single class of litigants or in a single class of cases. (See City of Alturas vs. the Superior Court of Modoc County (1940) 36 Cal.App.2nd 457. =(302/CWW/PB)
